stat_of_week: 40 RBI - North Correa's 40 RBI in a single week sets a new season record (previous mark was also 40, so this ties/sets the mark), headlined by Shohei Ohtani's 8 RBI and Brayan Rocchio's 7 RBI, yet the team could only manage a 5-5 tie
thriller_note: The Bombers survived on the strength of Daylen Lile's monster 4-HR, 10-RBI week and Zack Wheeler's near-perfect 0.63 ERA start, edging a Hiroshima squad that won pitching (more Ks, more QS, better ERA) but couldn't match the Bombers' explosive power output — a result that keeps Albuquerque afloat while Hiroshima's slide from the top-two power ranking conversation accelerates.
key_storyline: North Correa put up one of the most statistically dominant offensive weeks in league history — 40 RBI (a new season record), 31 runs, 10 stolen bases, a .394 OBP, and Shohei Ohtani anchoring the lineup with 8 RBI — and still couldn't beat Buttville Skidmarks, settling for a 5-5 tie. The tie is a brutal outcome for a team that desperately needs wins to climb the standings, and it perfectly encapsulates North Correa's season-long paradox: historic offensive production undermined by pitching and save/hold volatility. With a Week 9 matchup against General Hospital looming, this is a must-convert moment for a roster that keeps finding new ways to leave wins on the table.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers carry the league's highest luck delta at +1.70 and won 6-4 this week despite posting only 32 strikeouts and a negative NSVH, with Corbin Carroll's absurd .607 OBP papering over a roster with almost no pitching infrastructure.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs sport the league's worst luck delta at -0.99 and lost 6-3 this week despite Paul Skenes delivering 17 strikeouts and a 0.69 WHIP, a performance that on most rosters would anchor a winning effort.
records_broken: North Correa set a new season record with 40 RBI this week, matching the previous mark; Riverside Roughnecks set a new season record with 78 strikeouts by a pitching staff, surpassing the previous mark of 78 (tied at 78 — this is the new record). Note: most_rbi record listed as 40 is attributed to North Correa week 8, confirming the new record. Riverside Roughnecks' 78 Ks ties the most_k_team record of 78.0 set this same week.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Max Muncy's 9 runs, Drake Baldwin's 3 HR, a league-leading 36 runs scored, and 5 QS in an 8-2 demolition of General Hospital — this is the most complete roster in the league and it isn't particularly close | same

2. Riverside Roughnecks | A 7-1 blowout of Canberra backed by Nick Kurtz's 3 HR and 8 RBI, Roki Sasaki's 13 Ks, Jacob Misiorowski's 0.57 WHIP, and a season-record-tying 78 strikeouts signals this rotation has fully arrived | up

3. BaseballPro77 | Bobby Witt Jr., Austin Riley, and Munetaka Murakami each went deep twice in a 6-3 win; the offense finally has multiple credible power threats and the rotation held together well enough to get the job done | same

4. Athletic Park Dragons | Winning 5-4 despite a .296 OBP and only 39 hits is a testament to pitching depth — Cristopher Sánchez's 0.67 WHIP and Bailey Ober's 1.93 ERA anchored 4 QS — but the offense remains a genuine liability | same

5. Albuquerque Bombers | Daylen Lile's 4 HR and 10 RBI is a breakout performance that single-handedly won a close matchup, and Zack Wheeler's continued dominance makes this a dangerous team; the Max Meyer implosion (6.35 ERA) is the only real concern | up

6. North Correa | Setting a season RBI record with 40 and still only tying is the most North Correa outcome imaginable — the underlying talent is undeniable with Ohtani, Arozarena, and Bregman all producing, but one save/hold and shaky pitching depth keep costing them | down

7. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Juan Soto's 2 HR, Ben Rice's pop, and a solid 3.40 ERA aren't enough to overcome losing 6-4 to the Benchwarmers — a team with a -1 NSVH — which is a frustrating result for a squad with genuine upside | down

8. Hiroshima Radiation | Losing to the Bombers despite winning the pitching battle, 5 QS, and a 2.93 ERA is a damning result; the offense needs more than Schwarber and Harper carrying the load and this team is trending the wrong direction | down

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | The offensive numbers are legitimately good — 8 HR, 32 RBI, José Ramírez, Yordan Alvarez, Jazz Chisholm — but a 6.35 ERA and zero NSVH is a catastrophic pitching performance that needs immediate triage | same

10. Benchwarmers | Winning 6-4 with a negative NSVH and only 32 strikeouts while riding Corbin Carroll's .607 OBP is not a sustainable model; the luck delta of +1.70 is the highest in the league and this roster will be exposed when it regresses | down

11. General Hospital | Getting blown out 8-2 by the Skeeters when your staff posts a 3.18 ERA and 4 QS is an indictment of a lineup hitting .190 and posting a .270 OBP; the rotation is actually fine but there are simply not enough runs being scored | same

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Tying North Correa 5-5 when the opponent posts 40 RBI and 10 stolen bases is arguably the best result of the week on a per-talent basis; Cade Smith's 0.30 WHIP and 7 Ks in relief is a legitimate weapon, but the power numbers remain anemic | up

13. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Paul Skenes' 17 Ks are the best single-pitcher strikeout performance of the week and Colson Montgomery's 3 HR show real individual talent, but losing 6-3 to a BaseballPro77 team they should be competing with keeps the frustration running deep | same

14. Canberra Wombats | Getting blown out 7-1 despite Dylan Cease's 1.29 ERA is a damning indictment of an offense that could not generate runs against one of the hotter rosters in the league; this team needs a complete offensive overhaul and time is running short | same
waiver_highlights: Riverside Roughnecks | B+ | Added Walker Buehler (SP) and Ezequiel Duran (utility); dropped Kochanowicz and Manzardo | Buehler is a high-ceiling flier for a rotation that already looks dangerous, and Duran adds multi-position flexibility — smart depth moves from a team clearly building for a playoff run

North Correa | B | Added Gabriel Moreno (C) and Carmen Mlodzinski (SP/RP) in a catcher-heavy waiver frenzy; dropped Austin Wells, Nick Fortes, and Owen Caissie | The catcher carousel is dizzying but landing Moreno is a quality add; losing Caissie hurts outfield depth on a team that already needs more pop

Buttville Skidmarks | B- | Added Mark Vientos (1B/3B); dropped Nick Lodolo (SP) and Francisco Alvarez (C) | Vientos has real power upside and addresses the team's most glaring weakness, but dropping Lodolo gutts already-thin rotation depth and dropping Alvarez creates another hole

General Hospital | C+ | Added Brandon Young (SP) and Grant Holmes (SP); dropped Koenig and Rocker | Two speculative pitching adds to address a rotation that ironically doesn't need much help — the real problem is the lineup, and neither move does anything to fix a .270 OBP

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | C | Added Sean Newcomb and Jacob Latz (SP/RP); dropped Kyle Finnegan and both Tyler Mahle entries (apparent duplication error) | The Mahle confusion aside, Newcomb and Latz are low-upside backend arms that don't address the gap between this team's elite starters and its save/hold total

Albuquerque Bombers | C | Added Henry Bolte (OF); dropped Caglianone and Robby Snelling | Losing Snelling from a rotation with Max Meyer implosion concerns is a gamble; Bolte is a raw prospect add that won't contribute meaningfully in the short term
